title: pilosella floribunda subsp. suecica
intro: Pilosella floribunda subsp. suecica, also known as the Swedish mouse-ear or Swedish oxeye daisy, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family that is native to northern Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Pilosella floribunda subsp. suecica has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
